response:
## Perspective Audit Summary **Component/artifact reviewed:** Mega-Menu Navigation With Hover-Only Submenus **Perspectives escalated for deep review:** Keyboard Motor (HIGH), Screen Reader Semantic (MEDIUM), Cognitive Neurodivergent (MEDIUM) **Perspectives at LOW (not reviewed):** Magnification, Contrast, Motion, Auditory --- ### Findings by Severity | Severity | Count | |---|---| | CRITICAL | 1 | | MAJOR | 3 | | MINOR | 1 | | ENHANCEMENT | 0 | --- ### Perspectives That Passed None of the escalated perspectives passed all checklist items. --- ### Perspectives With Findings — Grouped by ARRM Role **Front-End Dev** - **Finding:** Submenus only accessible via hover, no keyboard activation (Keyboard Motor) — CRITICAL — 2.1.1 Keyboard Accessible - **Finding:** Submenu parent links lack `aria-expanded` and role="button" (Screen Reader) — MAJOR — 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value - **Finding:** Submenu visibility not announced to screen readers (Screen Reader) — MAJOR — 4.1.3 Status Messages **UX Design** - **Finding:** Submenu activation requires hover, creating inconsistency for keyboard users (Cognitive) — MAJOR — 3.2.4 Consistent Navigation **Content Author** - **Finding:** Submenu links lack descriptive text for screen reader context (Screen Reader) — MINOR — 2.4.4 Link Purpose --- ### Open Questions Requiring Human Verification None in this audit (all findings are code-level). --- ### Recommended Next Step **REVISE** (MAJOR findings present — address before shipping) --- ## Detailed Findings ### CRITICAL: Submenus only accessible via hover, no keyboard activation **WCAG:** 2.1.1 Keyboard Accessible **Perspective:** Keyboard Motor **Severity:** CRITICAL **Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: UX Design) **Evidence:** `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` used for submenu visibility, no keyboard event handlers **Fix:** Add `onFocus`/`onBlur` handlers to open/close submenus, ensure submenu links are in tab order ```jsx // Required fix example
  • setOpenMenu(item.id)} onMouseLeave={() => setOpenMenu(null)} onFocus={() => setOpenMenu(item.id)} // Add keyboard activation onBlur={() => setOpenMenu(null)} // Add keyboard deactivation > ``` --- ### MAJOR: Submenu parent links lack `aria-expanded` and role="button" **WCAG:** 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value **Perspective:** Screen Reader Semantic **Severity:** MAJOR **Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: Content Author) **Evidence:** `` elements used for parent links with children, no ARIA role or state **Fix:** Convert parent links to `role="button"` with `aria-expanded` state ```jsx { e.preventDefault(); setOpenMenu(openMenu === item.id ? null : item.id); }} > {item.label} ``` --- ### MAJOR: Submenu visibility not announced to screen readers **WCAG:** 4.1.3 Status Messages **Perspective:** Screen Reader Semantic **Severity:** MAJOR **Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: UX Design) **Evidence:** Submenu appears without `aria-live` announcement **Fix:** Add `aria-live="polite"` to submenu container and use `aria-atomic="true"` ```jsx
    {/* submenu links */}
    ``` --- ### MAJOR: Submenu activation requires hover, creating inconsistency **WCAG:** 3.2.4 Consistent Navigation **Perspective:** Cognitive Neurodivergent **Severity:** MAJOR **Route to:** UX Design (secondary: Front-End Dev) **Evidence:** Users must hover to access submenus, which is not keyboard-friendly **Fix:** Implement keyboard activation (see CRITICAL finding above) and add visual focus cues for submenu state --- ### MINOR: Submenu links lack descriptive text for screen reader context **WCAG:** 2.4.4 Link Purpose **Perspective:** Screen Reader Semantic **Severity:** MINOR **Route to:** Content Author (secondary: Front-End Dev) **Evidence:** Submenu links use only labels without context **Fix:** Add descriptive text to submenu links (e.g., "Widget Pro - Our flagship product") ```jsx {child.label} - {child.description} ``` --- ## Key Issues Summary ### Keyboard Motor (CRITICAL) - **Problem:** Submenus only accessible via hover, violating 2.1.1 - **Impact:** Users cannot navigate submenus with keyboard - **Fix Required:** Add keyboard activation and ensure submenu links are in tab order ### Screen Reader Semantic (MAJOR) - **Problem:** Missing ARIA roles/states for submenu interaction - **Impact:** Screen readers cannot announce submenu state changes - **Fix Required:** Add `role="button"`, `aria-expanded`, and `aria-live` ### Cognitive Neurodivergent (MAJOR) - **Problem:** Inconsistent navigation pattern between mouse and keyboard - **Impact:** Creates confusion for users with cognitive differences - **Fix Required:** Ensure consistent activation method across all input types --- ## Implementation Recommendations 1. **Keyboard Accessibility** - Replace `onMouseEnter` with `onFocus` for submenu activation - Ensure submenu links are in the tab order when open - Add `aria-expanded` to parent links 2. **Screen Reader Support** - Convert parent links to `role="button"` with `aria-expanded` - Add `aria-live` to submenu container for state announcements - Ensure all links have descriptive text 3. **Cognitive Consistency** - Implement consistent activation for all users - Add visual focus indicators for submenu state - Consider adding a "Expand submenu" button for keyboard users --- ## Verification Steps 1. Test keyboard navigation: - Tab through all top-level links - Verify submenus open on focus and close on blur - Ensure all submenu links are reachable via Tab 2. Test with screen reader: - Verify submenu state is announced when opened/closed - Check that all links have descriptive text - Confirm focus indicators are visible 3. Test with cognitive accessibility tools: - Ensure navigation is consistent across all input methods - Verify no unexpected layout shifts when submenus open - Check that all interactive elements are clearly labeled --- This audit identified critical accessibility issues that must be addressed before deployment. The component currently fails to meet basic keyboard and screen reader requirements, particularly around submenu accessibility. Implementing the recommended fixes will significantly improve accessibility for users with motor, visual, and cognitive disabilities.
